overallSummary: Leg A PASS (clone->Word reopen: hyperlink + table + image + bookmark all survived Word's round-trip, no repair prompt). Leg C PARTIAL (styled-table clone->Word: alignment + shading + merge survived, but Word DROPPED the table-style reference because the clone never emits the style DEFINITION in styles.xml). Leg B BLOCKED by Word session-instability quirks #24-26 (make-new-document authoring spawned spurious untitled windows + dangling references; native table+bookmark authoring never completed and was never saved). Word NEVER showed a repair/recovery prompt for any clone-authored file -> our OOXML is well-formed. Open+save-as round-trip path is fully healthy; only the make-new-document authoring path is broken in this session.
repairPromptNote: Neither roundtrip (Leg A nor Leg C) raised an AppleEvent error/timeout, and neither spawned an extra recovery document. The roundtrip verb returned ROUNDTRIP_OK both times. Word accepted the clone's OOXML cleanly.

legA.name: reopen-in-Word (clone -> Word, THE KEY TEST)
legA.input: /tmp/slice6-legA.docx (clone-exported: hyperlink->https://example.com + 3x4 table + embedded image + paired bookmark)
legA.method: word-oracle.js roundtrip: open in real Word -> save-as to slice6-legA-wordsaved.docx -> close (name-verified). Then docx-inspect on the Word-resaved file.

featuresSurvived.hyperlink: PASS - rId renumbered rId7->rId5, target https://example.com preserved exactly
featuresSurvived.image: PASS - 1 a:blip + 1 word/media file; 70 bytes preserved; Word renamed media to canonical word/media/image1.png
featuresSurvived.bookmark: PASS - paired bookmarkStart(id=0 name=spot1) + bookmarkEnd(id=0) preserved

normalizedByWord: relationship IDs renumbered (rId7->rId5 for the link, image rel renumbered to rId6) - benign, expected
image part renamed from media/image-2116158338.png to word/media/image1.png - benign, canonicalization

legB.name: Word-authored -> clone (native Word authoring, NO hyperlink)
legB.blockedBy: Word session-instability quirks #24-26 (scripts/oracle/README.md). 'make new document' authoring in this long-lived AppleScript session produced dangling document references (-1728 'object does not exist' / 'Can't get text range') and repeatedly spawned spurious untitled documents (Document3..Document8). Native table + bookmark authoring never completed; the legB scratch .docx was NEVER written to disk, so there was nothing to import into the clone.
legB.whatWasAttempted: 4 distinct AppleScript authoring strategies: (1) make new document + create range + make new table (table created but bookmark via 'text object:anchorRange' raised -1728); (2-3) corrected bookmark-properties syntax + save-as-by-var (dangling 'd' ref -> save as on wrong/renamed doc failed); (4) fully name-verified document-ref form with 90s timeout (-1728 on first table-dependent access). Each make-new-document call destabilized the session further.
legB.rootCauseRef: README quirk #24 (bare AppleEvent / make-new-document can spawn extra headless/untitled docs whose refs don't bind) + #26 (by-name/ordinal resolution flickers -1728 while the session is unsettled). Recovery prescription is a USER relaunch of Word, which is forbidden (never quit Word).
legB.whatWasValidatedInstead: Nothing for the Word->clone direction (no file produced). The clone's IMPORT of Word-authored tables+bookmarks is covered structurally by Leg A's reverse round-trip evidence (the clone-authored bookmark+table survive a full Word open/save cycle) but a true native-Word->clone import was not exercised.
legB.decisionNotToThrash: Per the task's ABSOLUTE SAFETY RULES, stopped after the session showed it was unhealthy (auto-spawning docs on nearly every interaction) rather than thrashing Word. Closed every scratch doc I created (Document3,5,6,7,8) by exact name; the session settled back to 3 untitled docs.

legC.name: 6b styled-table round-trip (clone -> Word)
legC.input: /tmp/slice6-legC.docx (clone-exported via WC.PM verbs: insertTable 3x3 + tableSetStyle('GridTable4-Accent1') + tableSetAlignment('center') + tableSetCellShading('FFFF00') over first-row pair + tableMerge first-row pair)
legC.method: word-oracle.js roundtrip (same healthy open+save-as path as Leg A) -> unzip-grep both the clone-exported file and the Word-resaved file.

preWord_attrs.tblStyle: <w:tblStyle w:val="GridTable4-Accent1"/>  (PRESENT but style is NOT defined in styles.xml)

postWord_attrs.jc: <w:jc w:val="center"/> (PRESERVED; Word writes it per-row, 4 occurrences)
postWord_attrs.shd: <w:shd w:val="clear" w:color="auto" w:fill="FFFF00"/> (PRESERVED + enriched to canonical form)

attrVerdicts.tblStyle: FAIL/DROPPED - Word discarded the GridTable4-Accent1 reference because the clone exporter emits the style ID on the table but never writes the style DEFINITION into word/styles.xml. Word drops a tblStyle reference whose definition is absent. This is a clone-exporter gap, NOT a Word quirk and NOT malformed OOXML (no repair prompt).
attrVerdicts.shd_shading: PASS - fill FFFF00 survived; Word normalized <w:shd w:fill=...> to the canonical <w:shd w:val="clear" w:color="auto" w:fill="FFFF00"/>

normalizedByWord: shd: clone's minimal <w:shd w:fill="FFFF00"/> expanded to canonical <w:shd w:val="clear" w:color="auto" w:fill="FFFF00"/> - benign enrichment
jc: re-emitted at per-row scope (4 occurrences) - benign
droppedByWord: w:tblStyle (GridTable4-Accent1) - dropped due to missing style definition in styles.xml (clone-side export gap to fix)

wordCleanliness.stateAtEnd: windows=3 documents=3 (Document1, Document2, Document4 - all untitled, none clone-named, none the user's saved/named work)
wordCleanliness.myScratchDocsClosed: All named round-trip scratch docs (slice6-legA-scratch.docx, slice6-legC-scratch.docx) were closed by the oracle's name-verified close. All make-new-document scratch docs I created (Document3,5,6,7,8) were closed by exact name.
wordCleanliness.residualConcern: Document2 and Document4 are untitled blank documents that appeared during Leg B's failed make-new-document authoring under the session-instability quirks. They were NOT closed because they cannot be safely attributed to my scratch-name set (PID-safety: only close docs created with my scratch names). They hold no content and are not the user's named files. Document1 is the original user baseline doc, untouched.

artifactsRemoved: slice6-legA-scratch.docx, slice6-legA-wordsaved.docx, slice6-legC-scratch.docx, slice6-legC-wordsaved.docx (repo-root scratch round-trip files - removed, not committed)
artifactsKept: scripts/slice6-legC-probe.js (permanent probe that builds the styled-table fixture)
